title: Sip, Sip, Hooray for Apple Cider Day!
story: Every November 18, people across America celebrate National Apple Cider Day by warming up with this delicious fall drink! Families visit apple orchards, cider mills, and farmers markets to buy fresh-pressed cider made from crisp apples. Some people heat it up with cinnamon and spices to make it extra cozy, while others enjoy it cold and refreshing. Coffee shops and restaurants often feature special cider drinks on their menus today. title: Soup-erb Cold Comfort on National Vichyssoise Day
story: Slurp up something surprising on November 18 — National Vichyssoise Day celebrates a soup that's meant to be eaten cold! title: Women's Golf Rivalry Tees Off
story: Golf balls soared as the first-ever Solheim Cup kicked off in 1990. This exciting tournament pitted top women golfers from the US against Europe. The American team, led by captain Kathy Whitworth, swung their way to victory. They outplayed their European rivals, captained by Mickey Walker, with a final score of 11½ to 4½ on November 18.

title: Zoilo Versalles: Shortstop Superstar
story: Zoilo Versalles dazzled baseball fans with his incredible skills at shortstop. In 1965, his outstanding performance for the Minnesota Twins earned him a special honor. title: Calvin Murphy's Free Throw Streak Ends
story: Swish! For 58 games, Calvin Murphy of the Houston Rockets sank free throws like clockwork. But on November 18, 1975, his incredible streak came to an end. Murphy, known for his accuracy at the foul line, had wowed basketball fans with his consistent shooting. title: Nirvana Unplugs for MTV
story: Grunge rockers Nirvana traded electric guitars for acoustic ones on November 18, 1993. The band filmed a special performance at Sony Music Studios in New York City. This wasn't just any concert - it was for MTV's popular 'Unplugged' series. Fans got to hear Nirvana's hard-hitting songs in a softer, more intimate way. The show became one of the most memorable in MTV history.
activity_question: What TV series was Nirvana's performance for?
bonus_fact: Nirvana's 'Unplugged' album won a Grammy for Best Alternative Music Album in 1996.

title: Rufus and Chaka Khan's Funky Triumph
story: Funk and soul exploded onto the music scene when Rufus featuring Chaka Khan released their fourth studio album on November 18, 1975. The band's electrifying sound and Khan's powerful vocals created a sensation. Their groovy tunes and catchy rhythms had everyone dancing. The album was so popular it became Billboard's Album of the Year for 1976!

bonus_fact: Chaka Khan is known as the Queen of Funk for her amazing voice and stage presence.

title: Genesis Unleashes a Rock Masterpiece
story: Rock fans rejoiced on November 18, 1974, as Genesis unveiled their epic album 'The Lamb Lies Down on Broadway'. This groundbreaking release marked the final time Peter Gabriel lent his distinctive voice to the band's progressive sound. Packed with intricate melodies and fantastical lyrics, the album took listeners on a mesmerizing musical journey.
activity_question: What was the name of the album Genesis released?

bonus_fact: Genesis began as a school band, with members meeting at Charterhouse School in England.
